campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help young people afford recreation programs. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a safe bicycle and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
City extends temporary ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17 gets approved for grant funding
Final feasibility study completed for Highway 17 crossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their views on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council moved to fast-track the ordinance by skipping its usual first reading process and scheduled it for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
Ordinance fast-tracked, skipping standard first reading
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council held a meeting to approve several housekeeping items, including final reports on the city's weed removal program and updated rules for how the council operates. The council also approved raising two new flags—Progress Pride and Juneteenth—to celebrate these occasions, heard from the regional transit authority about transportation plans, and discussed the city's housing goals and affordable housing policies.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New City Council operating procedures and norms
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
New flags for Progress Pride and Juneteenth celebrations
Regional transit and housing implementation plans

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with construction projects and professional services. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance program (CJPIA) to help manage the town's insurance costs.
keyTopics: New construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Updates to existing agreements with multiple vendors
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Art transfer agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to check on progress with youth and children's programs, including efforts to keep kids safe during summer and help young people get involved in their communities. They also reviewed how the city's code enforcement and library services are doing and what improvements are planned.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety programs for kids
Status update on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan
Code Enforcement Annual Report performance review
Library facility upgrades and customer service improvements

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation. Residents could join the virtual meeting to watch and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Meeting conducted with virtual/online option for residents
Public comment available via Zoom or phone dial-in
Instructions for raising hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by commissioners about life in Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony
Human Relations Commission meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 8, 2026 to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people. The council also approved minutes from their previous meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics within the city's jurisdiction.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer job opportunities for city residents
Approval of March meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online formats so residents could attend either way. The council provided information on how residents can watch the meeting via Zoom and submit written comments before the meeting takes place.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Zoom access details for remote participation
How to submit written public comments before the meeting
eComment submission process through City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a community education organization, and awarding a construction contract for a city project. The council also discussed updates to the city's housing and community development plans and reviewed new work requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining Franchise Tax Board partnership with county
Fee waiver and donation request from Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract award and project approval
Updates to 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New council member workplan item requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved reviewing financial forecasts, transferring reserves, and updating gas service rates for residential and commercial customers.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget approval for fiscal year 2027
Rate schedule changes for residential and commercial gas service
Reserve fund transfers and general fund adjustments
Financial forecast review for the gas utility
